title: pilocarpus racemosus var. mollis
intro: Pilocarpus racemosus var. mollis, also known as Jatamansi or Spiked Winter Cherry, is a plant species native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, characterized by its fragrant, woody roots and purple flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and sedative effects. The health benefits of Jatamansi include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of epilepsy and other neurological disorders. The plant can be prepared into various herbal formulations, including decoctions, powders, and essential oils, which are often used in traditional medicine and aromatherapy.
